result:
If I could choose one change, I would dissolve the illusion of separation — the belief that we are fundamentally divided by nation, ideology, identity, or species. I would nurture a deep, felt understanding that all life is interconnected, that another’s suffering is our own, and that the well-being of the whole is inseparable from our individual well-being.

In practical terms, that might mean dissolving the mental barriers that keep us from cooperating to solve our shared problems — climate crisis, injustice, poverty, conflict — not as “us versus them,” but as “all of us, together.”  

What would you choose?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
